subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #12 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Thank you!
Thank you to all our students and families for the lovely cards and treats. We so appreciate your kind words and beautiful pictures.
Thank you to PEP for organizing a teacher lunch on Thursday — we were absolutely full and happy! And thank you to everyone who brought in bagels, teas, cupcakes, and doughnuts throughout the week. Your generosity truly lifted our spirits.
A special acknowledgement to PEP for coordinating the Thanksgiving placement coloring project. Our students worked so thoughtfully on their 
placemats, and Ms. Jenny and the other teachers discussed the importance of helping others and how small acts of kindness can spread joy throughout our community.  These will be distributed through the OLS Thanksgiving Food Distribution this weekend.  The first two pictures above show Luca delivering the placements and the vast food organization required to serve the need in our community.  Thank you all.
Gardening with Ms. Linda
We did a lot of work this week, creating a new garden space around the oak tree at the entrance of our school. After spreading topsoil and compost we planted small Anemone blanda bulbs. Hopefully, they will yield little daisy-like, blue flowers in April and May.
We were also busy clearing dead plants out of our garden beds, putting the stalks in the compost and aerating the pile.  The kale is still thriving and we were 
able to harvest enough of it to make kale chips for next week’s Thanksgiving treat.
Before the holiday break, all the children will have a chance to bring home a purple coneflower seed head along with planting instructions. Coneflowers or Echinacea are native to North America and are an important source of nectar for butterflies and birds. The seeds can be planted now (they require cold winter temperatures before they are able to germinate in the spring). Through this effort, we hope to encourage the spread of pollinator-friendly plants throughout the community.

Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear for the day.  There is no bad weather just poor clothing.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water 
throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
